Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift
private struct HybridPlaceholderRow: View {
    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            // avatar circle
            Group {
                if let data = contact.imageThumbData, let img = UIImage(data: data) {
                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear
                }
            }
            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)
            .clipShape(Circle())
            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

---

## Implementation steps

   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrListView.swift`

   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsContactRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Services/ServicesRingOrderService.swift`

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

1→import Foundation
     2→import CoreData
     3→
     4→/// Handles drag-to-reorder logic with ring overflow cascade for BondEntity.
     5→/// When a bond is dragged to a new position, this service updates ringOrder values
     6→/// and cascades bonds to lower rings if needed. All mutations are perspective-specific (owner).
     7→class RingOrderService {
     8→    private let viewContext: NSManagedObjectContext
     9→    private let owner: ContactEntity
    10→
    11→    init(viewContext: NSManagedObjectContext, owner: ContactEntity) {
    12→        self.viewContext = viewContext
    13→        self.owner = owner
    14→    }
    15→
    16→    /// Reorder bond from old position to new position with cascade handling.
    17→    /// - Parameters:
    18→    ///   - bond: Bond being moved
    19→    ///   - fromRingOrder: Current ringOrder position
    20→    ///   - toRingOrder: New ringOrder position
    21→    /// - Returns: List of bonds whose ring changed due to cascade
    22→    func reorderBond(
    23→        _ bond: BondEntity,
    24→        from fromRingOrder: Int16,
    25→        to toRingOrder: Int16
    26→    ) throws -> [BondEntity] {
    27→        guard fromRingOrder != toRingOrder else { return [] }
    28→        let allBonds = try fetchAllBondsSorted()
    29→        guard let currentIndex = allBonds.firstIndex(where: { $0.objectID == bond.objectID }) else {
    30→            return []
    31→        }
    32→
    33→        let targetIndex = clampIndex(for: toRingOrder, total: allBonds.count)
    34→        if currentIndex == targetIndex { return [] }
    35→
    36→        var reordered = allBonds
    37→        let movedBond = reordered.remove(at: currentIndex)
    38→        reordered.insert(movedBond, at: targetIndex)
    39→
    40→        var affected: [BondEntity] = []
    41→        let now = Date()
    42→        for (index, item) in reordered.enumerated() {
    43→            let expectedRingOrder = Int16(index + 1)
    44→            let expectedRing = Ring.from(ringOrder: expectedRingOrder).rawValue
    45→            if item.ringOrder != expectedRingOrder || item.ring != expectedRing {
    46→                item.ringOrder = expectedRingOrder
    47→                item.ring = expectedRing
    48→                item.updatedAt = now
    49→                affected.append(item)
    50→            }
    51→        }
    52→
    53→        return affected
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    /// Apply a full ordered list of bonds and rewrite ringOrder/ring in one pass.
    57→    /// Used by native `.onMove` reorder flows.
    58→    @discardableResult
    59→    func applyOrderedBonds(_ orderedBonds: [BondEntity]) -> [BondEntity] {
    60→        var affected: [BondEntity] = []
    61→        let now = Date()
    62→
    63→        for (index, item) in orderedBonds.enumerated() {
    64→            let expectedRingOrder = Int16(index + 1)
    65→            let expectedRing = Ring.from(ringOrder: expectedRingOrder).rawValue
    66→            if item.ringOrder != expectedRingOrder || item.ring != expectedRing {
    67→                item.ringOrder = expectedRingOrder
    68→                item.ring = expectedRing
    69→                item.updatedAt = now
    70→                affected.append(item)
    71→            }
    72→        }
    73→
    74→        return affected
    75→    }
    76→
    77→    /// Fetch all bonds owned by this owner, globally ordered by ringOrder.
    78→    private func fetchAllBondsSorted() throws -> [BondEntity] {
    79→        let request: NSFetchRequest<BondEntity> = BondEntity.fetchRequest()
    80→        request.predicate = NSPredicate(format: "owner == %@", owner)
    81→        request.sortDescriptors = [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)]
    82→        return try viewContext.fetch(request)
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    /// Convert 1-indexed ringOrder target into a safe 0-indexed array position.
    86→    private func clampIndex(for ringOrder: Int16, total: Int) -> Int {
    87→        let rawIndex = Int(ringOrder) - 1
    88→        return min(max(rawIndex, 0), max(total - 1, 0))
    89→    }
    90→}
    91→

```swift
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`

2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift

    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {

                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))

        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch

   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)

```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
```

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera

        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`

```swift
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            // avatar circle
            Group {
                if let data = contact.imageThumbData, let img = UIImage(data: data) {
                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear
                }
            }
            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)
            .clipShape(Circle())
            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift
private struct HybridPlaceholderRow: View {
    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {

                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {

            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode

```swift
@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)

        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.

    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`

Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift
private struct HybridPlaceholderRow: View {
    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            // avatar circle
            Group {
                if let data = contact.imageThumbData, let img = UIImage(data: data) {
                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear
                }
            }
            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)
            .clipShape(Circle())
            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

---

## Implementation steps

1. Read these files first to understand current code:
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrListView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/ContentView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsConnectionSheet.swift` (if it exists)
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsContactRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Services/ServicesRingOrderService.swift`

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

3. Modify `ContentView.swift` per the plan

4. Modify `ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift` per the plan

5. Modify `NavigationRoute.swift` to remove `case rings`

6. Delete `ViewsRingsView.swift`

7. Run a build to verify: `xcodebuild -scheme souvenir -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15 Pro' build 2>&1 | tail -50`

8. Fix any compile errors until the build is clean

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Read each file carefully before modifying
- The `Route` enum case `rings` should be removed from NavigationRoute.swift, but any remaining references in other files must also be cleaned up
- Check if `Route.rings` is referenced in ContentView.swift's navigation switch and remove that case
- `BondEntity` has `ringOrder` attribute and `owner` relationship to `ContactEntity`
- `ContactEntity` has `isSelf` attribute and `bonds` relationship
- `RingOrderService` takes `viewContext` and `owner` (a ContactEntity)
- After build is clean, verify no warnings about unused variables or unreachable code

Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift
private struct HybridPlaceholderRow: View {
    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            // avatar circle
            Group {
                if let data = contact.imageThumbData, let img = UIImage(data: data) {
                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear
                }
            }
            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)
            .clipShape(Circle())
            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

---

## Implementation steps

1. Read these files first to understand current code:
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrListView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/ContentView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsConnectionSheet.swift` (if it exists)
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsContactRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Services/ServicesRingOrderService.swift`

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

3. Modify `ContentView.swift` per the plan

4. Modify `ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift` per the plan

5. Modify `NavigationRoute.swift` to remove `case rings`

6. Delete `ViewsRingsView.swift`

7. Run a build to verify: `xcodebuild -scheme souvenir -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15 Pro' build 2>&1 | tail -50`

8. Fix any compile errors until the build is clean

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Read each file carefully before modifying
- The `Route` enum case `rings` should be removed from NavigationRoute.swift, but any remaining references in other files must also be cleaned up
- Check if `Route.rings` is referenced in ContentView.swift's navigation switch and remove that case
- `BondEntity` has `ringOrder` attribute and `owner` relationship to `ContactEntity`
- `ContactEntity` has `isSelf` attribute and `bonds` relationship

Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift

    let contact: ContactEntity

        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }

```

---

## Implementation steps

1. Read these files first to understand current code:
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrListView.swift`

   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsConnectionSheet.swift` (if it exists)
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsContactRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Services/ServicesRingOrderService.swift`

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift
private struct HybridPlaceholderRow: View {
    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            // avatar circle
            Group {
                if let data = contact.imageThumbData, let img = UIImage(data: data) {
                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear
                }
            }
            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)
            .clipShape(Circle())
            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/ContentView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsConnectionSheet.swift` (if it exists)
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsContactRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Services/ServicesRingOrderService.swift`

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

3. Modify `ContentView.swift` per the plan

4. Modify `ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift` per the plan

5. Modify `NavigationRoute.swift` to remove `case rings`

6. Delete `ViewsRingsView.swift`

7. Run a build to verify: `xcodebuild -scheme souvenir -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15 Pro' build 2>&1 | tail -50`

8. Fix any compile errors until the build is clean
