Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift
private struct HybridPlaceholderRow: View {
    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            // avatar circle
            Group {
                if let data = contact.imageThumbData, let img = UIImage(data: data) {
                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear
                }
            }
            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)
            .clipShape(Circle())
            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

data.prompt:
Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift
private struct HybridPlaceholderRow: View {
    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            // avatar circle
            Group {
                if let data = contact.imageThumbData, let img = UIImage(data: data) {
                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear
                }
            }
            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)
            .clipShape(Circle())
            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

---

## Implementation steps

1. Read these files first to understand current code:
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrListView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/ContentView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsConnectionSheet.swift` (if it exists)
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsContactRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Services/ServicesRingOrderService.swift`

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

3. Modify `ContentView.swift` per the plan

4. Modify `ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift` per the plan

5. Modify `NavigationRoute.swift` to remove `case rings`

6. Delete `ViewsRingsView.swift`

7. Run a build to verify: `xcodebuild -scheme souvenir -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15 Pro' build 2>&1 | tail -50`

8. Fix any compile errors until the build is clean

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Read each file carefully before modifying
- The `Route` enum case `rings` should be removed from NavigationRoute.swift, but any remaining references in other files must also be cleaned up
- Check if `Route.rings` is referenced in ContentView.swift's navigation switch and remove that case
- `BondEntity` has `ringOrder` attribute and `owner` relationship to `ContactEntity`
- `ContactEntity` has `isSelf` attribute and `bonds` relationship
- `RingOrderService` takes `viewContext` and `owner` (a ContactEntity)
- After build is clean, verify no warnings about unused variables or unreachable code

input.command: xcodebuild -scheme souvenir -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15 Pro' build 2>&1 | grep -E "error:|warning:" | head -30

Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift

    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {

                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear

            }

            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

---

## Implementation steps

1. Read these files first to understand current code:
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrListView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/ContentView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsConnectionSheet.swift` (if it exists)
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsContactRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Services/ServicesRingOrderService.swift`

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

5. Modify `NavigationRoute.swift` to remove `case rings`

6. Delete `ViewsRingsView.swift`

7. Run a build to verify: `xcodebuild -scheme souvenir -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15 Pro' build 2>&1 | tail -50`

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift
private struct HybridPlaceholderRow: View {
    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            // avatar circle
            Group {
                if let data = contact.imageThumbData, let img = UIImage(data: data) {
                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear
                }
            }
            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)
            .clipShape(Circle())
            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Views/ViewsContactRingsView.swift`
   - `/Users/kerry/house/desk/SVNR/souvenir/Services/ServicesRingOrderService.swift`

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)

```swift
    Spacer()

        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt

                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)

        Image(systemName: "camera")

.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

content.text:
Build is clean. Here is a summary of everything changed.

---

## What was implemented

### New file: `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`

Unified root view for both list modes:

- **Timeline mode** — `@FetchRequest` on `SouvenirEntity`, sorted by frecency via `SouvenirEntity.byFrecencyDescending`. Renders `SouvenirCapsuleView` rows inside a `List`. Includes the trailing `AddSouvenirCapsuleView` placeholder and `AmbientGlyph` footer. Shows `SouvenirOnboardingIntroView` when empty.
- **Rings mode** — `@FetchRequest` on `BondEntity` filtered to `owner.isSelf == YES`, sorted by `ringOrder`. Renders `SouvenirCapsuleView` for contacts with a souvenir, `HybridPlaceholderRow` for those without. Tapping a placeholder opens `RingsConnectionSheet`.
- **Drag-to-reorder** — `.onMove` is wired only in rings mode. Uses `RingOrderService.applyOrderedBonds` + `viewContext.save()`, matching the pattern from the deleted `RingsView`.

toolUseResult.prompt:
Implement the following plan for the SVNR iOS app. Read the relevant existing files first, then implement all changes.

# Plan: SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List

## Context
The current app has two separate views for the same data: SvnrListView (timeline sort, souvenirs only) and RingsView (ringOrder sort, all bonded contacts). The user wants a single unified view that handles both sort modes, large SouvenirCapsuleView rows, swipe-to-branch navigation, and a redesigned 3-button toolbar that eliminates the sliding MapListToggle pill.

---

## Architecture

### ViewMode semantics (unchanged enum, changed meaning of `.rings`)
- `.timeline` → hybrid view, sorted by souvenir `createdAt` descending, souvenirs-only
- `.rings` → hybrid view, sorted by `BondEntity.ringOrder`, all bonded contacts (with placeholder for no souvenir)
- `.map` → SvnrMapView (unchanged)

`.rings` is no longer a navigation destination; it's a root view mode like `.timeline`. `Route.rings` in NavigationRoute stays but its destination in ContentView is removed.

---

## Files to Create

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
Replaces SvnrListView as the root content view for both timeline and rings modes.

**Two fetch requests:**
```swift
// Timeline mode: souvenirs sorted by createdAt
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \SouvenirEntity.createdAt, ascending: false)], ...)
private var souvenirs: FetchedResults<SouvenirEntity>

// Rings mode: self's bonds sorted by ringOrder
@FetchRequest(sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
              predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>

@FetchRequest(predicate: NSPredicate(format: "isSelf == YES"), ...)
private var selfContacts: FetchedResults<ContactEntity>
```

**Body structure:**
```swift
ZStack {
    BackgroundView(image: favoriteBackgroundImage)
    List {
        ForEach(items, id: \.id) { item in
            rowView(for: item)
                .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
                .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
                .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
                .swipeActions(edge: .trailing, allowsFullSwipe: false) {
                    Button { router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: item.contactID)) } label: {
                        Label("View Tree", systemImage: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
                    }
                    .tint(.accent)
                }
        }
        .onMove(perform: viewMode == .rings ? moveBonds : nil)
    }
    .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    // Always .constant(.active) — never toggled, so no re-layout jitter.
    // Drag handles only appear when .onMove is non-nil (rings mode).
    // Swipe actions work in both modes regardless of edit mode state.
    .environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))
}
```

**Row logic:**
- If contact has a primarySouvenir → `SouvenirCapsuleView(souvenir:, onSelect:, transitionNamespace:)` (reuse existing, no changes needed)
- If no souvenir (rings mode only) → `HybridPlaceholderRow(contact:)` — thin card with avatar + displayName + "No memory yet" secondary text; same height/insets as capsule for visual consistency

**Drag-to-reorder:** `.onMove` calls `RingOrderService(viewContext:owner:).applyOrderedBonds(_:)` + `viewContext.save()`, identical to current RingsView pattern.

**Background loading:** Same `PhotoLoadingService` + `ColorExtractionService` pattern as SvnrListView — load favorite souvenir photo for background.

---

## Files to Modify

### `souvenir/ContentView.swift`
1. Replace `SvnrListView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` with `SvnrHybridView(viewMode:transitionNamespace:)` in rootContent
2. Add the condition: show SvnrHybridView when `viewMode == .timeline || viewMode == .rings`
3. Remove `case .rings: RingsView(viewMode:)` from `destinationView` switch
4. `toolbarIsVisible`: simplify to `hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera && router.currentRoute == nil`
   (No longer needs `|| router.currentRoute == .rings` since rings is root-level now)
5. Update `toolbarRingsAction` → `toolbarCenterAction`: toggle between `.timeline` and `.rings`
6. Add `toolbarMapAction`: toggle to `.map` or return to last list mode
7. Add `toolbarCameraAction`: post `.svnrRequestCamera` notification

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
Redesign from 3-element to clean 3-button layout. Remove MapListToggle.

**New interface:**
```swift
struct PersistentToolbar: View {
    @Binding var viewMode: MainViewMode
    let onMapAction: () -> Void
    let onCenterAction: () -> Void   // toggles timeline/rings when on list; returns to list when on map
    let onCameraAction: () -> Void
}
```

**Layout:**
```swift
HStack {
    // Leading: map
    Button(action: onMapAction) {
        Image(systemName: viewMode == .map ? "list.bullet" : "map")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
    Spacer()
    // Center: glyph + SVNR
    Button(action: onCenterAction) {
        HStack(spacing: 5) {
            // reuse the SvnrGlyphLineTraceView or Image("svnr-glyph-fill") at 22pt
            Image("svnr-glyph-fill")
                .resizable().scaledToFit().frame(width: 22, height: 22)
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
            Text("SVNR")
                .font(.headline.weight(.heavy).design(.rounded))
                .foregroundStyle(viewMode == .rings ? .accent : .primary)
        }
    }
    Spacer()
    // Trailing: camera
    Button(action: onCameraAction) {
        Image(systemName: "camera")
            .frame(width: 44, height: 44)
    }
}
.frame(height: 44)
// ... existing background/shadow styling
```

The glyph tints `.accent` when in `.rings` mode to indicate manual sort is active.

### `souvenir/Views/NavigationRoute.swift`
Remove `case rings` from `Route` enum — it was only used to navigate to the now-removed RingsView destination. All ring interactions now happen in the root hybrid view. (The `contactRings` route stays for tree navigation.)

### `souvenir/Views/ViewsRingsView.swift`
Delete file — RingsView is replaced by SvnrHybridView.

---

## What Stays Unchanged
- `SouvenirCapsuleView` — used as-is inside the List rows; no parameter changes needed
- `BondListRow` — only used in ContactRingsView now (tree navigation), untouched
- `RingOrderService` — reused directly in moveBonds
- `ContactRingsView` — unchanged; still used for recursive tree navigation via onBranchTap
- `SvnrMapView` — unchanged
- `MapListToggle` — removed from toolbar; keep file but it becomes unused

---

## HybridPlaceholderRow (new, small, in ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
```swift
private struct HybridPlaceholderRow: View {
    let contact: ContactEntity
    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            // avatar circle
            Group {
                if let data = contact.imageThumbData, let img = UIImage(data: data) {
                    Image(uiImage: img).resizable().scaledToFill()
                } else {
                    Color.clear
                }
            }
            .frame(width: 48, height: 48)
            .clipShape(Circle())
            .background(Circle().fill(.ultraThinMaterial))

            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.displayName).font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
                Text("No memory yet").font(.caption).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
            }
            Spacer()
        }
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .background(.ultraThinMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16))
    }
}
```

---

## Implementation steps

1. Read these files first to understand current code:

2. Create `ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift` based on the plan above, adapting actual code patterns from the existing files

3. Modify `ContentView.swift` per the plan

6. Delete `ViewsRingsView.swift`

7. Run a build to verify: `xcodebuild -scheme souvenir -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15 Pro' build 2>&1 | tail -50`

IMPORTANT NOTES:

- **Rings mode** — `@FetchRequest` on `BondEntity` filtered to `owner.isSelf == YES`, sorted by `ringOrder`. Renders `SouvenirCapsuleView` for contacts with a souvenir, `HybridPlaceholderRow` for those without. Tapping a placeholder opens `RingsConnectionSheet`.

# SVNR Project Memory

## BondEntity — Directional Ring Relationships (2026-02-27)
✅ Implemented perspective-specific ring organization via `BondEntity`:
- **New entity**: BondEntity (join table) with directed owner→contact edges plus ring/ringOrder
- **Schema (v4)**: Two inverse relationships on ContactEntity: `bonds` (I own) for queries, `bondsIn` (point to me) for referential integrity
- **Attributes**: id, ring, ringOrder (Int16), createdAt, updatedAt all required with defaults in awakeFromInsert
- **Core Data files**: ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass.swift, ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift
- **Refactored RingOrderService**: Now owns a ContactEntity (the perspective) and mutates BondEntity records (ring, ringOrder, updatedAt)
- **ViewsRingsView**: Fetches BondEntity where owner.isSelf==YES; groupByRing; passes selfContact to RingOrderService
- **ViewsContactRingsView fix**: Loads trunk/limbBonds by querying "owner == %@" instead of global ContactEntity.ring (FIXES BUG where navigating to Josh showed Kerry's rings)
- **DevTools**: Added BondEntity to clearAllData batch delete (before ContactEntity for referential integrity)
- **Seed data**: Added Kerry as isSelf=true ContactEntity + 21 BondEntity records (Kerry→21 contacts, distributed across trunk/limb/leaf)
- **Build**: Clean, no errors; lightweight migration transparent
- **No breaking changes**: ContactEntity.ring/ringOrder remain optional (soft-deprecated), all queries now BondEntity

## Communication Preferences
**Never say "Perfect"** — it's a hollow filler word. Be specific about what was actually accomplished instead of using generic affirmations.

## Burst Capture + Auto Best-Frame Selection (2026-02-26)
✅ Implemented silent rolling buffer burst with automatic quality selection:
- New file: `souvenir/Services/ServicesFrameQualityAnalyzer.swift` — actor scoring frames via:
  - Sharpness: Laplacian edge kernel convolution + pixel variance (0.5 weight)
  - Face detection: VNDetectFaceLandmarksRequest (baseline 0.15)
  - Eyes detection: landmark presence scoring (0.35 weight)
- Refactored `CameraController` in ViewsCustomCameraView.swift:
  - **Rolling buffer**: Continuously collects video frames into a 45-frame circular buffer (~1.5s at 30fps)
  - **Zero latency**: `captureBurst()` grabs pre-captured frames, no new captures needed
  - **Live Photo approach**: Analyzes the entire rolling window, picks best frame
  - Replaced AVCapturePhotoOutput with AVCaptureVideoDataOutput (silent)
  - Changed from `AVCapturePhotoCaptureDelegate` to `AVCaptureVideoDataOutputSampleBufferDelegate`
- All callers unchanged — `onCapture` still receives `(UIImage, CLLocation?)` tuple
- **No shutter sounds** — silent video frame extraction
- **Instant feedback**: Frames already exist before user taps, analysis is the only latency (~200-300ms)

## UI Fixes Completed (2026-02-25)
- ✅ ViewsSvnrGlyph.swift: Added `import UIKit` for haptic feedback generators
- ✅ LocationSearchIsland: Enabled GlassSearchField's built-in clear button (`showClear: true`)
- ✅ LocationSearchIsland: Changed dismiss button icon from xmark to chevron.down
- ✅ ContentView.swift: Added `import UIKit`
- ✅ ViewsSouvenirView: Repositioned PhotoMetadataPill to last item in ZStack (floats above ContactIsland)
- Build verified: All changes compile successfully

**AmbientGlyph implementation note:** Full touch interaction already implemented with drag gestures, velocity calculation, inertia physics (friction 0.93), haptic feedback, and 2-second auto-return to origin. Only missing `import UIKit` which is now fixed.

## Core Data delete validation order
All four entities have `validateForDelete` guards that form a dependency web:
- `SouvenirEntity`: throws if `hasActiveShares`
- `ContactEntity`: throws if `primarySouvenir != nil`
- `PhotoEntity`: throws if `favoritedBySouvenir != nil` or is souvenir's only photo

**Fix pattern for bulk delete (e.g. DevTools nuke):**
Phase 1 — clear blocking relationships on all SouvenirEntity (`favoritePhoto`, `primaryContact`, `photos`, `shares` = nil), then `save()`.
Phase 2 — delete all entities in order, then `save()`. Context-based deletion so NSPersistentCloudKitContainer tracks deletes to iCloud.

## Issues.md protocol
- `[ ]` open
- `[-]` fix implemented, ready to test
- `[x]` tested and confirmed closed
- `[+]` needs another look
Do NOT mark `[x]` — leave that for the user after testing.

## CloudKit setup
Container: `iCloud.ink.kerry.souvenir`, development environment.
To manually clear CloudKit data: icloud.developer.apple.com/dashboard → Development.
User's test device is their everyday phone — signing out of iCloud is not viable.

## Orfx constellation
Identities live in `~/house/keep/orfx/`. Summon by @handle:
@vega (INTENT), @nakai (EXPERIENCE), @prince (PRODUCT), @yvon (BRAND),
@cyd (SYSTEMS), @sophie (QUALITY), @riane (FEEDBACK),
@djehuti (CHRONICLE), @dudamel (CONDUCTOR), @wesley (SCOUT), @mawlana (FOOL)
To spawn as a role: role doc (`~/house/keep/orfx/roles/<ROLE>.md`) + `SUBAGENT.md` as system prompt.
Subagents leave artifacts, reply `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`, phone home to CONDUCTOR if lost.

## Commit style
Attribute agent work collectively as the Orphics — the constellation of agents (Claude, Codex, Worfeus, etc.):
Co-Authored-By: the Orphics <orfx@agentmail.to>

## Version bumping
Version lives in `souvenir.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` (not Info.plist).
- `MARKETING_VERSION` — display version (e.g. 0.1, 1.0)
- `CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION` — build number
Both appear multiple times (Debug/Release configs); bump all occurrences.
Include the version bump in the commit when asked to commit after a bump.

## Key patterns
- `photosArray` on SouvenirEntity uses `objc_getAssociatedObject` cache; call `invalidatePhotosCache()` after mutations.
- Carousel navigation after photo add: use `pendingNewPhotoID: UUID?` state + `onChange(of: pendingNewPhotoID)` to avoid race with `onChange(of: photos.count)` → `syncLoopedIndexFromCurrent()`.
- Email placeholder blue color: ZStack with explicit `Text(...).foregroundStyle(Color(UIColor.placeholderText))` overlaid behind an empty-string TextField; `prompt:` approach is overridden by iOS email content-type coloring.

input.new_string:
## BondEntity — Directional Ring Relationships (2026-02-27)
✅ Implemented perspective-specific ring organization via `BondEntity`:
- **New entity**: BondEntity (join table) with directed owner→contact edges plus ring/ringOrder
- **Schema (v4)**: Two inverse relationships on ContactEntity: `bonds` (I own) for queries, `bondsIn` (point to me) for referential integrity
- **Attributes**: id, ring, ringOrder (Int16), createdAt, updatedAt all required with defaults in awakeFromInsert
- **Core Data files**: ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass.swift, ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift
- **Refactored RingOrderService**: Now owns a ContactEntity (the perspective) and mutates BondEntity records (ring, ringOrder, updatedAt)
- **ViewsRingsView DELETED**: Replaced by SvnrHybridView (2026-02-28)
- **ViewsContactRingsView**: Loads trunk/limbBonds by querying "owner == %@"; used for tree navigation via contactRings route
- **DevTools**: Added BondEntity to clearAllData batch delete (before ContactEntity for referential integrity)
- **Seed data**: Added Kerry as isSelf=true ContactEntity + 21 BondEntity records (Kerry→21 contacts, distributed across trunk/limb/leaf)
- **Build**: Clean, no errors; lightweight migration transparent
- **No breaking changes**: ContactEntity.ring/ringOrder remain optional (soft-deprecated), all queries now BondEntity

## SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List (2026-02-28)
✅ Replaced ViewsSvnrListView + ViewsRingsView with a single unified root view:
- **File**: `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
- **Timeline mode**: FetchRequest on SouvenirEntity sorted by frecency; SouvenirCapsuleView rows; AddSouvenirCapsuleView + AmbientGlyph footer
- **Rings mode**: FetchRequest on BondEntity (owner.isSelf==YES) sorted by ringOrder; SouvenirCapsuleView for contacts with souvenir, HybridPlaceholderRow (tap → RingsConnectionSheet) for those without
- **Drag-to-reorder**: `.onMove` wired only in rings mode; calls `RingOrderService.applyOrderedBonds` + save
- **Swipe-to-branch**: Both modes; swipe left → `.contactRings(contactID:)` via router
- **Edit mode always active**: `.environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))` — drag handles appear in rings mode without toggling state; swipe actions work in both
- **Background loading**: Same PhotoLoadingService + ColorExtractionService pattern
- **Route.rings REMOVED** from Route enum — rings is now a root ViewMode, not a navigation destination
- **MainViewMode.rings** still exists in ViewsBottomNavBar.swift (it's the view mode enum, different from Route)

## Toolbar Redesign — 3-Button Layout (2026-02-28)
✅ Replaced MapListToggle sliding pill with clean 3-button PersistentToolbar:
- **File**: `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
- **Interface**: `onMapAction`, `onCenterAction`, `onCameraAction` — no more embedded toggle logic
- **Layout**: map icon | svnr-glyph-fill + "SVNR" | camera icon — all tappable, equal Spacer between
- **Rings mode indicator**: center glyph+wordmark tints `.accent` when `viewMode == .rings`
- **Map button**: shows `list.bullet` icon when in map mode (return affordance), `map` icon otherwise
- **MapListToggle file kept** but no longer used by anything

## `import UIKit` in SwiftUI files — DON'T (2026-02-28)
SwiftUI imports UIKit transitively on iOS. Adding `import UIKit` explicitly to a SwiftUI file causes a **cascading SourceKit error storm** that makes unrelated types (Router, MainViewMode, CoreData entities) appear missing across many files. The actual `xcodebuild` compiler is fine, but SourceKit goes haywire.
- **Rule**: Never add `import UIKit` to a `.swift` file that already imports `SwiftUI`
- **Exception**: Files that don't import SwiftUI (e.g., pure UIKit subclasses, custom camera layers) can import UIKit directly
- **Fix**: Remove the explicit `import UIKit` — SourceKit clears on next index cycle after the offending import is removed

## BondEntity — Directional Ring Relationships (2026-02-27)
✅ Implemented perspective-specific ring organization via `BondEntity`:
- **New entity**: BondEntity (join table) with directed owner→contact edges plus ring/ringOrder
- **Schema (v4)**: Two inverse relationships on ContactEntity: `bonds` (I own) for queries, `bondsIn` (point to me) for referential integrity
- **Attributes**: id, ring, ringOrder (Int16), createdAt, updatedAt all required with defaults in awakeFromInsert
- **Core Data files**: ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass.swift, ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift
- **Refactored RingOrderService**: Now owns a ContactEntity (the perspective) and mutates BondEntity records (ring, ringOrder, updatedAt)
- **ViewsRingsView DELETED**: Replaced by SvnrHybridView (2026-02-28)

- **DevTools**: Added BondEntity to clearAllData batch delete (before ContactEntity for referential integrity)

- **Build**: Clean, no errors; lightweight migration transparent
- **No breaking changes**: ContactEntity.ring/ringOrder remain optional (soft-deprecated), all queries now BondEntity

## SvnrHybridView — Unified Timeline/Rings List (2026-02-28)
✅ Replaced ViewsSvnrListView + ViewsRingsView with a single unified root view:
- **File**: `souvenir/Views/ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift`
- **Timeline mode**: FetchRequest on SouvenirEntity sorted by frecency; SouvenirCapsuleView rows; AddSouvenirCapsuleView + AmbientGlyph footer
- **Rings mode**: FetchRequest on BondEntity (owner.isSelf==YES) sorted by ringOrder; SouvenirCapsuleView for contacts with souvenir, HybridPlaceholderRow (tap → RingsConnectionSheet) for those without
- **Drag-to-reorder**: `.onMove` wired only in rings mode; calls `RingOrderService.applyOrderedBonds` + save
- **Swipe-to-branch**: Both modes; swipe left → `.contactRings(contactID:)` via router
- **Edit mode always active**: `.environment(\.editMode, .constant(.active))` — drag handles appear in rings mode without toggling state; swipe actions work in both
- **Background loading**: Same PhotoLoadingService + ColorExtractionService pattern
- **Route.rings REMOVED** from Route enum — rings is now a root ViewMode, not a navigation destination
- **MainViewMode.rings** still exists in ViewsBottomNavBar.swift (it's the view mode enum, different from Route)

## Toolbar Redesign — 3-Button Layout (2026-02-28)
✅ Replaced MapListToggle sliding pill with clean 3-button PersistentToolbar:
- **File**: `souvenir/Views/ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift`
- **Interface**: `onMapAction`, `onCenterAction`, `onCameraAction` — no more embedded toggle logic
- **Layout**: map icon | svnr-glyph-fill + "SVNR" | camera icon — all tappable, equal Spacer between
- **Rings mode indicator**: center glyph+wordmark tints `.accent` when `viewMode == .rings`
- **Map button**: shows `list.bullet` icon when in map mode (return affordance), `map` icon otherwise
- **MapListToggle file kept** but no longer used by anything

## `import UIKit` in SwiftUI files — DON'T (2026-02-28)
SwiftUI imports UIKit transitively on iOS. Adding `import UIKit` explicitly to a SwiftUI file causes a **cascading SourceKit error storm** that makes unrelated types (Router, MainViewMode, CoreData entities) appear missing across many files. The actual `xcodebuild` compiler is fine, but SourceKit goes haywire.
- **Rule**: Never add `import UIKit` to a `.swift` file that already imports `SwiftUI`
- **Exception**: Files that don't import SwiftUI (e.g., pure UIKit subclasses, custom camera layers) can import UIKit directly
- **Fix**: Remove the explicit `import UIKit` — SourceKit clears on next index cycle after the offending import is removed

# SVNR Project Memory

## BondEntity — Directional Ring Relationships (2026-02-27)
✅ Implemented perspective-specific ring organization via `BondEntity`:
- **New entity**: BondEntity (join table) with directed owner→contact edges plus ring/ringOrder
- **Schema (v4)**: Two inverse relationships on ContactEntity: `bonds` (I own) for queries, `bondsIn` (point to me) for referential integrity
- **Attributes**: id, ring, ringOrder (Int16), createdAt, updatedAt all required with defaults in awakeFromInsert
- **Core Data files**: ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass.swift, ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift
- **Refactored RingOrderService**: Now owns a ContactEntity (the perspective) and mutates BondEntity records (ring, ringOrder, updatedAt)
- **ViewsRingsView**: Fetches BondEntity where owner.isSelf==YES; groupByRing; passes selfContact to RingOrderService
- **ViewsContactRingsView fix**: Loads trunk/limbBonds by querying "owner == %@" instead of global ContactEntity.ring (FIXES BUG where navigating to Josh showed Kerry's rings)
- **DevTools**: Added BondEntity to clearAllData batch delete (before ContactEntity for referential integrity)
- **Seed data**: Added Kerry as isSelf=true ContactEntity + 21 BondEntity records (Kerry→21 contacts, distributed across trunk/limb/leaf)
- **Build**: Clean, no errors; lightweight migration transparent
- **No breaking changes**: ContactEntity.ring/ringOrder remain optional (soft-deprecated), all queries now BondEntity

## Communication Preferences
**Never say "Perfect"** — it's a hollow filler word. Be specific about what was actually accomplished instead of using generic affirmations.

## Burst Capture + Auto Best-Frame Selection (2026-02-26)
✅ Implemented silent rolling buffer burst with automatic quality selection:
- New file: `souvenir/Services/ServicesFrameQualityAnalyzer.swift` — actor scoring frames via:
  - Sharpness: Laplacian edge kernel convolution + pixel variance (0.5 weight)
  - Face detection: VNDetectFaceLandmarksRequest (baseline 0.15)
  - Eyes detection: landmark presence scoring (0.35 weight)
- Refactored `CameraController` in ViewsCustomCameraView.swift:
  - **Rolling buffer**: Continuously collects video frames into a 45-frame circular buffer (~1.5s at 30fps)
  - **Zero latency**: `captureBurst()` grabs pre-captured frames, no new captures needed
  - **Live Photo approach**: Analyzes the entire rolling window, picks best frame
  - Replaced AVCapturePhotoOutput with AVCaptureVideoDataOutput (silent)
  - Changed from `AVCapturePhotoCaptureDelegate` to `AVCaptureVideoDataOutputSampleBufferDelegate`
- All callers unchanged — `onCapture` still receives `(UIImage, CLLocation?)` tuple
- **No shutter sounds** — silent video frame extraction
- **Instant feedback**: Frames already exist before user taps, analysis is the only latency (~200-300ms)

## UI Fixes Completed (2026-02-25)
- ✅ ViewsSvnrGlyph.swift: Added `import UIKit` for haptic feedback generators
- ✅ LocationSearchIsland: Enabled GlassSearchField's built-in clear button (`showClear: true`)
- ✅ LocationSearchIsland: Changed dismiss button icon from xmark to chevron.down
- ✅ ContentView.swift: Added `import UIKit`
- ✅ ViewsSouvenirView: Repositioned PhotoMetadataPill to last item in ZStack (floats above ContactIsland)
- Build verified: All changes compile successfully

**AmbientGlyph implementation note:** Full touch interaction already implemented with drag gestures, velocity calculation, inertia physics (friction 0.93), haptic feedback, and 2-second auto-return to origin. Only missing `import UIKit` which is now fixed.

## Core Data delete validation order
All four entities have `validateForDelete` guards that form a dependency web:
- `SouvenirEntity`: throws if `hasActiveShares`
- `ContactEntity`: throws if `primarySouvenir != nil`
- `PhotoEntity`: throws if `favoritedBySouvenir != nil` or is souvenir's only photo

**Fix pattern for bulk delete (e.g. DevTools nuke):**
Phase 1 — clear blocking relationships on all SouvenirEntity (`favoritePhoto`, `primaryContact`, `photos`, `shares` = nil), then `save()`.
Phase 2 — delete all entities in order, then `save()`. Context-based deletion so NSPersistentCloudKitContainer tracks deletes to iCloud.

## Issues.md protocol
- `[ ]` open
- `[-]` fix implemented, ready to test
- `[x]` tested and confirmed closed
- `[+]` needs another look
Do NOT mark `[x]` — leave that for the user after testing.

## CloudKit setup
Container: `iCloud.ink.kerry.souvenir`, development environment.
To manually clear CloudKit data: icloud.developer.apple.com/dashboard → Development.
User's test device is their everyday phone — signing out of iCloud is not viable.

## Orfx constellation
Identities live in `~/house/keep/orfx/`. Summon by @handle:
@vega (INTENT), @nakai (EXPERIENCE), @prince (PRODUCT), @yvon (BRAND),
@cyd (SYSTEMS), @sophie (QUALITY), @riane (FEEDBACK),
@djehuti (CHRONICLE), @dudamel (CONDUCTOR), @wesley (SCOUT), @mawlana (FOOL)
To spawn as a role: role doc (`~/house/keep/orfx/roles/<ROLE>.md`) + `SUBAGENT.md` as system prompt.
Subagents leave artifacts, reply `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`, phone home to CONDUCTOR if lost.

## Commit style
Attribute agent work collectively as the Orphics — the constellation of agents (Claude, Codex, Worfeus, etc.):
Co-Authored-By: the Orphics <orfx@agentmail.to>

## Version bumping
Version lives in `souvenir.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` (not Info.plist).
- `MARKETING_VERSION` — display version (e.g. 0.1, 1.0)
- `CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION` — build number
Both appear multiple times (Debug/Release configs); bump all occurrences.
Include the version bump in the commit when asked to commit after a bump.

## Key patterns
- `photosArray` on SouvenirEntity uses `objc_getAssociatedObject` cache; call `invalidatePhotosCache()` after mutations.
- Carousel navigation after photo add: use `pendingNewPhotoID: UUID?` state + `onChange(of: pendingNewPhotoID)` to avoid race with `onChange(of: photos.count)` → `syncLoopedIndexFromCurrent()`.
- Email placeholder blue color: ZStack with explicit `Text(...).foregroundStyle(Color(UIColor.placeholderText))` overlaid behind an empty-string TextField; `prompt:` approach is overridden by iOS email content-type coloring.

# AGENTS.md - Who We Are & How We Work

**See `/Users/kerry/house/keep/AGENTS.md` for our shared identity, rituals, and operating principles.**

This file contains SVNR-specific notes for agents working on this iOS app.

## SVNR-Specific Operating Notes

### Fix Philosophy
- **Surgical, minimal changes.** Preserve all existing behavior unless explicitly changing it.
- **Follow SwiftUI patterns.** Use native idioms (@State, @Binding, onChange, etc.) over custom solutions.
- **Test incrementally.** Build after each change; verify on device when touching UI/performance-sensitive code.
- **Substantial changes require a build.** After any substantial code edits, run an Xcode build and fix any new build errors before continuing.

### Constraint: The Four Entities
Contact, Photo, Souvenir, Share are **PROTECTED**. Never delete entities or attributes. Consult `souvenir/Docs/PHASE0_ModelsSCHEMA.md` before any schema changes.

### Domain Rules (Quick Reference)
- One Souvenir per primary Contact
- Photos many-to-many Contacts
- Photos many-to-one Souvenir
- favoritePhoto drives Polaroid front face
- Phone numbers stored in E.164 format
- Frecency: `log1p(photoCount) × exp(-λ × daysSinceLastPhoto)`, λ = ln(2)/14

### Testing
- Swift Testing framework for unit tests (`@Test`, `#expect`, `@Suite`)
- XCTest for UI tests (required for XCUIApplication)
- In-memory Core Data via `/dev/null` store URL for unit tests

### Build & Deploy
```bash
# Build for device
xcodebuild -scheme souvenir -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,name=Handfill' build

# Or use Fish function
runios
```

### Phase Work
Follow `docs/ROADMAP.md` phase sequence. Mark phases complete (✓) or in-progress (🚧) as work proceeds. Update estimated vs. actual time, files created/modified.

**For general agent behavior, memory patterns, rituals, and constellation work:**  
See `/Users/kerry/house/keep/AGENTS.md`

The fix is just removing the import.
+- **Exception:** Files without `import SwiftUI` (pure UIKit subclasses, AVFoundation layers, etc.) may import UIKit directly.

# README - SVNR Project Context

Start here. This file is the quick ramp for agents.

See `docs/CONSTITUTION.md` for response protocol and core values.
See `AGENTS.md` for who we are and how we operate together.
See `docs/skills/` for agent guidance on specific workflows (e.g., `log-status.md` for recording session progress).

# Response style
- Minimal explanation unless asked
- Provide working code when writing code
- No boilerplate comments

# Project: SVNR - Context
This is an iOS app called "souvenir" (bundle ID: ink.kerry.souvenir)

# Beta focus (must-know)
We are shipping a narrow social feature for beta launch:
- Shared Souvenir (2-party): either party can add photos; both see updates
- CloudKit-backed sync
- Private and non-reshareable
- UX lives inside `SouvenirView`

See `docs/roadmap.md` for full Trust Forest MVP + beta scope.
See `docs/shared-souvenir-plan.md` for the beta implementation plan.
See `docs/technical-notes.md` for consolidated engineering notes.
See `docs/cloudkit-sharing.md` for CloudKit integration notes.

# Code style
- Swift: prefer value types, protocol-oriented design
- No force unwraps
- Explicit types for public interfaces
- Default to native SwiftUI/Apple frameworks (MapKit, SwiftUI components, system services) and embellish as needed; only roll custom UI/services when adapting native options is more work than building and maintaining a bespoke solution.
- Documentation lives in code: use terse doc blocks per method (purpose + parameters + common use).
- Keep comments current with behavior; update Previews with UI changes.
- Add tests for new or failure-prone services.
- Sheet translucency: use `.presentationBackground(.clear)` and provide your own full-bleed `.ultraThinMaterial` background inside the sheet content; clear UIKit table backgrounds (`UITableView/UITableViewCell`) for List/Form.

# Workflow
- Test build on physical device "Handfill" or simulator "iPhone 17 Pro"
```
xcodebuild -scheme souvenir -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,name=Handfill' build
```
- Use Fish shell functions for build/deploy

# Architecture & Domain
- SwiftUI + Core Data + CNContactStore + PHPhotoLibrary + MapKit
- Four entities (PROTECTED): Contact, Photo, Souvenir, Share - never delete
- One Souvenir per primary Contact; Photos many-to-many Contacts
- Frecency: log1p(photoCount) × exp(-λ × daysSinceLastPhoto), λ = ln(2)/14
- Phone numbers in E.164 format; favoritePhoto drives Polaroid front

# File naming
- Models: Models*+CoreDataClass.swift
- Views: Views*.swift (e.g. ViewsSouvenirListView.swift)
- Services: Services*.swift
- Tests: Tests*.swift

# Testing
- Swift Testing framework preferred (@Test, #expect, @Suite)
- In-memory Core Data via /dev/null for unit tests
- Protocol-based DI with fakes for testability
- Previews of key interactions/interfaces for UI testing

# Current status
- Phase 0-16 complete ✓
- Phase 17 in progress 🎨
- See `docs/roadmap.md` for phase sequence and beta scope
- See `docs/issues.md` for bugs
- Issues protocol:
  - `[ ]` open
  - `[-]` fix implemented, ready for you to test
  - `[x]` tested and confirmed closed
  - `[+]` needs another look

# Priority issues
- Location search map (fuzzy search + MapKit integration)
- ContactIsland progressive enhancement (hide fields until name entered)
- Photo carousel position indicator unclear

content.thinking:
Now I need to understand what changes belong to which grouping. Let me look at the git log to understand what commits were made since the last clean state (we're 32 commits ahead of origin).

Actually wait - the user wants me to commit the *current unstaged/staged changes* into sensible groups. These aren't previous commits - these are the working tree changes.

Let me think about the groupings:

1. **BondEntity schema + RingOrderService refactor** (minor bump 0.2)
   - New Core Data schema v4 with BondEntity
   - ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass/Properties (new files)
   - SouvenirModel v4 contents and .xccurrentversion
   - ModelsContactEntity+CoreDataProperties (bonds/bondsIn relationships)
   - ServicesRingOrderService refactor
   - Seed data updates
   - CoreDataJSONExport/Import updates

2. **SvnrHybridView unified list** (minor bump 0.3)
   - ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift (new)
   - ViewsRingsView.swift (deleted)
   - ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift (new, redesigned)
   - NavigationRoute.swift (removed)
   - Route.rings, ContentView.swift (routing changes)
   - ViewsContactRingsView (bug fix)
   - ViewsBottomNavBar.swift

3. **Other changes** - there are many modified files, so I need to figure out which changes belong to which feature. Let me check git diff --stat to get a better sense of the scope. Actually, there are a lot of files here, so I need to categorize them properly. The BondEntity work was committed in 84b50b0 with the Rings MVP, but those files are still showing as modified, which means there were further changes after that commit. Let me look at git diff for specific files to understand what actually changed since the last commit.

Actually, I'm thinking about this wrong. The git status is showing all files modified since the last commit, which was just adding design/ to .gitignore. So all these changes represent work since that point. Looking back at the history, 84b50b0 implemented the Rings MVP with BondEntity and RingOrderService, but those files are still appearing as modified now, which means there's been additional work on top of that.

Let me check what's actually changed from HEAD using a diff to understand the real state of things. I'm noticing the SouvenirModel.xcdatamodeld file has both A and M status, meaning it was staged as new but then modified again after staging—this is the v4 schema version.

Looking at the staged files, there's a new ViewsSvnrToast.swift file ready to go. But the untracked files are interesting—the BondEntity CoreData class and properties files haven't been committed yet, along with a couple of new view files like ViewsPersistentToolbar and ViewsSvnrHybridView. This tells me the 84b50b0 commit was probably an earlier checkpoint, not the complete picture.

Now I need to figure out how to group these changes logically. The BondEntity files and the schema update seem to go together as one cohesive change around the data model refactor.

Organizing the commits into logical groups...

I'm realizing there are too many modified files to categorize manually—I need to look at the actual diff output to see which files changed and group them properly by feature.

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(data): BondEntity directed ring relationships (v0.2)

Introduces BondEntity as the join table for perspective-specific ring
organization. Ring membership is now a directed edge from an owner
ContactEntity to a contact ContactEntity, replacing the flat ring/ringOrder
attributes on ContactEntity itself.

### Core Data schema (v4)
- New BondEntity with attributes: id (UUID), ring (String), ringOrder
  (Int16), createdAt, updatedAt — all required with defaults in awakeFromInsert
- ContactEntity gains two inverse relationships: `bonds` (bonds I own)
  and `bondsIn` (bonds pointing to me); old ring/ringOrder fields kept
  but soft-deprecated
- v3 model patched to mark ring/ringOrder as optional for lightweight
  migration; v4 model is the active version

### ModelsBondEntity
- ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass.swift: awakeFromInsert sets id/timestamps;
  `displayName` computed from contact; `ring` computes to Ring enum
- ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift: NSManaged attributes and
  owner/contact relationships

### ModelsContactEntity
- Added @NSManaged bonds: Set<BondEntity>? and bondsIn: Set<BondEntity>?
- displayName computed accessor updated

### ServicesRingOrderService
- Refactored from actor to class; now owns a `ContactEntity` (perspective)
- All reorder mutations target BondEntity records (ring, ringOrder, updatedAt)
  rather than the deprecated ContactEntity fields
- applyOrderedBonds(_:) replaces the old index-based API

### CoreData JSON export/import
- CoreDataJSONExport: BondEntity serialization added
- CoreDataJSONImport: BondEntity deserialization with owner/contact lookups

### Seed data
- seed.json: Kerry added as isSelf=true root ContactEntity
- 21 BondEntity records wiring Kerry to seed contacts across
  trunk (5), limb (8), and leaf (8) rings

### Views
- ViewsDevTools: clearAllData now batch-deletes BondEntity before
  ContactEntity to respect referential integrity
- ViewsContactRingsView: rewritten with @FetchRequest on BondEntity
  (predicate: owner.id == contactID) instead of ContactEntity.ring;
  fixes the bug where navigating to any contact showed Kerry's rings
- ViewsRingsConnectionSheet: fetches contactBonds to display the
  visited contact's ring organization inside the connection sheet

Co-Authored-By: the Orphics <orfx@agentmail.to>
EOF
)"

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(nav): SvnrHybridView unified list + 3-button PersistentToolbar (v0.3)

Replaces the separate SvnrListView and RingsView with a single unified root
view (SvnrHybridView) that handles both timeline and rings modes. Redesigns
the toolbar from a sliding MapListToggle pill to a clean 3-button layout.

### SvnrHybridView (new: ViewsSvnrHybridView.swift)
- Timeline mode: FetchRequest on SouvenirEntity sorted by frecency;
  renders SouvenirCapsuleView rows, AddSouvenirCapsuleView trailing
  placeholder, and AmbientGlyph footer
- Rings mode: FetchRequest on BondEntity (owner.isSelf == YES) sorted
  by ringOrder; SouvenirCapsuleView for contacts with a souvenir,
  HybridPlaceholderRow (tap → RingsConnectionSheet) for those without
- Drag-to-reorder: .onMove wired in rings mode only; delegates to
  RingOrderService.applyOrderedBonds + viewContext.save()
- Swipe-to-branch: both modes; swipe left → .contactRings(contactID:)
- Edit mode always active (.constant(.active)) so drag handles appear
  in rings mode without toggling; swipe actions work in both modes
- Background loading: same PhotoLoadingService + ColorExtractionService
  pattern as SvnrListView; palette extracted from favorite souvenir photo
- HybridPlaceholderRow: avatar circle + displayName + "No memory yet";
  same padding/insets as SouvenirCapsuleView for visual consistency

### ViewsRingsView deleted
- Fully replaced by SvnrHybridView; no functionality lost

### PersistentToolbar redesigned (ViewsPersistentToolbar.swift)
- Old interface (MapListToggle pill + onLeadingAction/onRingsAction)
  replaced with clean 3-button layout
- Leading: map icon (list.bullet when in map mode) via onMapAction
- Center: svnr-glyph-fill + "SVNR" wordmark; tints .accent when in
  rings mode to signal manual-sort is active
- Trailing: camera icon via onCameraAction
- Notification name svnrShouldHideContactSheet moved here from ContentView

### ContentView updated
- rootContent: SvnrHybridView replaces SvnrListView for .timeline and .rings
- toolbarIsVisible: simplified to hasSavedFirstSouvenir && !showCamera &&
  router.currentRoute == nil (no longer needs || .rings special case)
- toolbarMapAction: toggles .map on/off, returns to stored list mode
- toolbarCenterAction: toggles .timeline/.rings; returns to .timeline from map
- toolbarCameraAction: posts .svnrRequestCamera after 0.35s delay
- storedViewModeRaw @AppStorage used to remember last list mode across sessions

### NavigationRoute
- Route.rings case removed; rings is now a root MainViewMode, not a
  navigation destination. Route.contactRings remains for tree navigation.

### Supporting changes
- ViewsBottomNavBar: MainViewMode gains .rings case (was missing)
- ViewsMapListToggle: guard viewMode != .rings in drag/toggle handlers
  so the old toggle ignores rings mode (file kept but no longer in toolbar)
- ViewsSvnrGlyph: onGlyphTap callback added to AmbientGlyph; external
  color props (glyphNextColor) now override internal color cycle
- ViewsSvnrListView: wires onGlyphTap to toggle showAmbientGlyphLineTrace

Co-Authored-By: the Orphics <orfx@agentmail.to>
EOF
)"

fix(share): stable CloudKit share token URLs (v0.3.2)

Older ShareEntity rows stored "cloudkit.zoneshare" as their token, which is
not unique per souvenir and produced colliding share URLs. This fix derives
a stable token from cloudKitRootRecordName when the raw token is the
generic sentinel value.

### ServicesShareService
- effectiveToken(for:): private helper that prefers cloudKitRootRecordName
  over the raw token when token == "cloudkit.zoneshare"
- shareURL(for:) and shareURL(for:includeMetadata:) now call effectiveToken
  and percent-encode the result consistently
- testFlightURL replaced with testFlightURL: String? = nil (set when live)
  and testerSignupURL fallback pointing to svnr.kerry.ink
- appStoreURL placeholder removed

### ViewsComponentsShareSheet
- Added shareSheetLoadingPlaceholder branch for .invite/.activity modes
  when shareEntity is nil (prevents blank sheet flicker on slow init)
- Removed redundant cloudKitShareURL fallback in loadExistingShare; all
  paths now route through shareService.shareURL(for:)

### ViewsAdminDualInviteView
- After share creation, resolves the display URL through
  shareService.activeShares + shareService.shareURL(for:) rather than
  using the raw URL returned by CloudKit (which may differ from the
  stable token URL shown to users)

Co-Authored-By: the Orphics <orfx@agentmail.to>
EOF
)"
